_comment: INJECTED for #881 — do NOT overwrite on re-record. The first attempt at this scene's primary image returns a content-checker 422 (sequenceIndex 0); the image workflow's CF default step retry re-runs and the success (no sequenceIndex, so it matches every later call) lands, so the frame still ends thumbnailStatus=completed. This exercises the content-flag retry inside the full pipeline — the existing 'every frame completed' assertion proves the rescue. The no-index success (rather than sequenceIndex 1) keeps the full-sequence test retriable in CI: on a Playwright retry the error fixture is already consumed, so the scene just succeeds. If you re-run `bun test:e2e:full:record`, re-apply this two-entry shape (error first).

match.userMessage:
Cinematic realistic still of SCARLETT at a sunlit vanity, fingers beginning to lift the lid of the white box with potential energy in the motion, modern interior vanity table bathed in bright natural window light casting clean directional shadows across soft neutral surfaces, high-key airy atmosphere with warm sunlight highlights and lifted shadows, eye-level angle with shallow depth of field emphasizing tactile details on the box, modern social-first product photography style with crisp whites and true-to-life color grading.

  <reference-images>
    - Image 1 [CHARACTER REF]: Scarlett - Match the real-world appearance of Sienna Blake exactly
    - Image 2 [LOCATION REF]: INT. VANITY ROOM - DAY - Sunlit vanity with a white box
    
    IMPORTANT: Reference images CARRY the visual identity of their labeled objects. When the prompt names an UPPERCASE element token, render it faithfully from its ELEMENT REF image — do not generate a new version from the prose. When the prompt names a LOCATION REF, use that image for the environment, lighting, and architectural identity. Prose describes how things are framed, lit, and positioned in the shot — it does not redefine what the reference already shows.
  </reference-images>

error.message: The content could not be processed because it contained material flagged by a content checker.
